---
# ======================================================================
# Reference-format test harness for the jacrquarto extension.
# Renders every tricky reference type so the output can be compared to the
# JACR Guidelines for Authors. Not part of a real manuscript.
#   quarto render reference-test.qmd --to jacrquarto-pdf
# ======================================================================
title: "Reference Format Test"
jacr-mode: manuscript
format:
  jacrquarto-pdf: default
bibliography: bibliography.bib
# nocite forces every entry into the reference list even if not cited above.
nocite: |
  @belk2013, @wansink2013, @taylor1981, @lastovicka1979, @anderson1986,
  @wallendorf1987, @simmons1986, @census1983, @doe2000, @frank1999,
  @veblen1979, @scammon2011, @lasaleta2014, @sameyear1988a, @sameyear1988b
---

# In-Text Citation Tests

Single author: [@belk2013]. Two authors, always both: [@taylor1981]. Two
authors named in a sentence [@lastovicka1979].

Three authors, first mention should list all three: [@lasaleta2014]. Three
authors, second mention should collapse to "et al.": [@lasaleta2014]. Four or
more authors, always "et al.": [@hibbard2004]; and a nine-author work
[@scammon2011].

Page locator, no "p." [@veblen1979, 25]. Multiple citations, listed
chronologically and separated by semicolons [@taylor1981; @belk2013;
@scammon2011]. Same author and year, disambiguated by letter
[@sameyear1988a; @sameyear1988b].

# Reference List

The list below should reproduce the JACR reference formats: year in
parentheses after the authors, unabbreviated periodical names in italics,
issue by month, no "p." on page ranges, book and chapter formats with place
and publisher, and the reprint shown as 1899/1979.
